dialogos.texto_en: Oh, Futaro-kun?\nIt's surprising to see you interested\nin this kind of shop.
Ah! Did you perhaps come to see me?
No, that's not it.\nI just happened to see you guys going in,\nso I stopped by.
Come on, you don't have to make excuses.\nYou really came after us, didn't you?
Well, um...
Does that mean I'm bothering you guys?
Don't be ridiculous.\nAnyway, what were you looking at?
I was looking at shirts and some small items.\nThey have a great selection with good taste.
There are also many interesting things.\nLet's look around together!
Fine, I guess.\nWhat were you looking at, Nino?
Masking tape. They have many different kinds here?
Tape?
Look, like this.\nYou've seen something like this before, right?
This one with prints of animals and plants\nfrom Okinawa?
It's one of the shop's original products.
Hey, what's this used for?
Decorating a notebook, adding accents to gift wrapping,\nor using it for interior design, for example.
Nino, you're good at that kind of stuff, huh.
But masking tape isn't meant to be used\nfor those purposes, is it?
Yeah, it was originally intended as protective tape\nto prevent things from getting dirty, wasn't it?
These days, though, there are a lot of masking tapes\nthat are designed with a focus on aesthetics.
If you use them cleverly to add a bit of variety,\neven ordinary things can become fashionable in no time.
And what about this beautiful colored one?\nDon't you think it would look so pretty\ndisplayed in an acrylic case?
Huh? You're going to display it without even using it?\nDoes it have to be tape for that?
You need to start thinking more flexibly.
No, that's not the issue here.
Hmm, what to do.\nI like this one, but I like this one too.
Ohh, let me see.
This one's a blue color inspired by the sea in Okinawa,\nbut it's painful to let go of this sky blue one.
Ah, for sure, this is a tough decision.
Well, let's put the blue ones aside for now\nand go with something like this floral print.\nIs this hibiscus?
D-don't confuse me!
(Are they struggling to choose\n which Okinawa-themed tape to buy?)
Why buy what you have no use for?\nIn the end, it'll just be a waste, won't it?
Now listen here, Futaro-kun.\nThat kind of waste is needed in life.
Do you think you just said something\nwise just now?
You're wrong.\nIt seems that you still lack\nlife experience, Futaro-kun.
These things can raise your spirits\nor be nourishing for your heart.
By the way, if you had to choose one\ncolor from these, which one would you prefer, Fu-kun?
I think any of them would be the same, but...\nif I had to choose,\nmaybe this sea-blue color would be nice.
Okay, then I'll buy you the one you pick out, Fu-kun.
Huh?\nAre you sure about that?
This tape has a beautiful shade of ocean blue.
Oh, what made that color stand out to you?
What made the color stand out...?
Perhaps it's because we played\nin the sea yesterday.\nThat vivid blue left quite an impression on me.
Ah! I can see that!
I saw the sea from both underwater while snorkeling\nand from the sky, and everything was beautiful.
Besides, you can't buy anything else\nwith an Okinawan ocean motif.\nThey say it was the store's original design.
That's right. These might be perfect as a mementos.\nShould we go with these?
Okay, then I'll buy you the one you pick out, Fu-kun.
Huh?\nAre you sure about that?
You should think about what to do with it,\nand then choose based on that.
What do you mean by 'what to do' with it?\nYou wouldn't put it in a case?
That's not what I mean.\nFor example%p-2;――
If we place this floral pattern with the sky blue\nand this ocean blue, don't you get a sense\nof Okinawa's land, sea, and sky?
Wow...
Mmm...
What's with THOSE reactions?
I was thinking it's unusual for you\nto say something so stylish, Futaro-kun.
Even you, Fu-kun, have good taste.\nI feel like I've lost somehow.
Are you complimenting me?
I am.\nCome on, don't pout like that.
I'm not pouting!
Okay, then I'll buy you the one you pick out, Fu-kun.
Huh?\nAre you sure about that?
What's the issue?
Even though you were thinking about it so much....\nAre you sure it's okay to go with something\nI chose on a whim?
Absolutely.\nWhat's important is that YOU choose it, Fu-kun.
I'll make sure to display it carefully,\nso come and see it in my room next time, okay?
Huh? A-alright...
Teehee. I'll go buy it, then.
(I wonder if there's any deeper meaning...\n to 'come and see it in my room'...)
What's wrong, Futaro-kun?
N-no, it's nothing.
Are you sure?\nYou have a troubled look, I'd say.
I'm telling you, I'm fine.\nWell, I'll try going to a different store.
Okay, see you later.
